facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 110 hours at 1000 Hz over 2.4 GHz; up to 150 hours over Bluetooth
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims, not live capacity telemetry.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: Synapse polling is limited to 125 Hz, calibration is disabled, and onboard profiles are stored from the 2.4 GHz route
notes: Bluetooth is a direct product route and is not carried by the HyperFlux mat.

facts.label: Default DPI stages
notes: Stages are customizable in Razer Synapse.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: These are product/Synapse capabilities, not evidence of firmware-native HyperFlux commands.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: 13 zones across the scroll wheel, logo, and full underglow

facts.label: Profile storage
facts.value: 4 onboard plus 1 active profile

facts.label: Polling rate
notes: 8000 Hz requires the Mouse Dock Pro and HyperPolling Wireless Dongle.

facts.label: Scroll behavior
facts.value: Four-way tilt wheel with electronically actuated tactile and free-spin modes

facts.label: Sensor calibration
facts.value: Surface calibration, Smart Tracking, asymmetric cut-off, and lift-off/landing distances
notes: Razer documents calibration as unavailable through Bluetooth.

facts.label: Sleep timeout
facts.value: Configurable wireless idle timeout

gaps.title: Bluetooth control is not established
gaps.detail: Official Bluetooth product support does not establish an exact Linux control path for this model. Bluetooth is outside the HyperFlux receiver route and remains unclaimed.

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: The model is on the manufacturer candidate list, but no exact receiver-backed child profile currently authorizes controls.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 140 hours at 1000 Hz over 2.4 GHz; up to 28 hours at 8000 Hz; up to 210 hours over Bluetooth
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims, not live capacity telemetry; 8000 Hz requires the Mouse Dock Pro or HyperPolling Wireless Dongle.

facts.label: Reported Bluetooth HID identity
notes: This community-reported Bluetooth identity differs from the Razer USB identities and grants no HyperFlux control authority.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: Synapse polling is limited to 125 Hz, calibration is disabled, and onboard profiles are stored from the 2.4 GHz route
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: DPI stages
facts.value: Up to five configurable stages with independent X/Y sensitivity

facts.label: Official HyperFlux role
facts.value: Compatible charging mouse and receiver client
notes: Manufacturer compatibility does not itself authorize a Linux receiver command; this exact model has separate physical HyperFlux qualification.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: These are product/Synapse capabilities, not evidence of firmware-native HyperFlux commands.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: 13 zones: scroll wheel, logo, and 11-zone multi-zone underglow
notes: OpenRGB calls the underglow group LED Strip; the product-facing name is multi-zone underglow.

facts.label: Polling rate
notes: 8000 Hz requires the Mouse Dock Pro or HyperPolling Wireless Dongle.

facts.label: Scroll behavior
facts.value: Tactile, free-spin, Smart-Reel, and configurable scroll acceleration

facts.label: Sensor calibration
facts.value: Surface calibration, Smart Tracking, asymmetric cut-off, and lift-off/landing distances
notes: Razer documents calibration as unavailable through Bluetooth.

facts.label: Sleep timeout
facts.value: Configurable wireless idle timeout

gaps.title: Bluetooth Linux control is not established
gaps.detail: The upstream report records a distinct Bluetooth HID identity and missing OpenRazer recognition. HyperFlux does not carry or authorize this direct Bluetooth route.

gaps.title: Advanced mouse controls are not HyperFlux-mapped
gaps.detail: DPI, scroll behavior, calibration, sleep timeout, and low-battery configuration are documented product capabilities, but the current receiver profile does not authorize those writes.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 200 hours with backlighting off; published examples range from about 6 to 25 hours with backlighting on
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims, not live capacity telemetry; lighting color, effect, and brightness materially change runtime.

facts.label: Bluetooth Quick Effects
notes: Studio, Audio Visualizer, and onboard-memory lighting are unavailable over Bluetooth.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: No Chroma Studio, limited Chroma effects, and no onboard-memory support
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: The separately documented Bluetooth subset remains listed under Bluetooth Quick Effects.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: Individually backlit per-key RGB

facts.label: Low-battery notification
notes: The keyboard repeats its critical-battery lighting sequence below this threshold.

facts.label: Media controls
facts.value: Four dedicated controls plus a multi-function digital dial

facts.label: Onboard profiles
notes: Razer documents onboard-memory features as unavailable over Bluetooth.

facts.label: Wireless power policy
facts.value: Configurable dim and sleep timeouts

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: The model is a manufacturer-listed candidate, but no exact receiver-backed child profile currently authorizes controls.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 980 hours in Power Saving Mode
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims and depend on reduced polling, disabled lighting, and paused Synapse connectivity.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: No Chroma Studio, limited Chroma effects, and no onboard-memory support
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: Lighting
facts.value: Razer Chroma RGB
notes: The reviewed product sources do not specify exact application matrix geometry.

facts.label: On-device lighting shortcuts
notes: These shortcuts are product behavior, not HyperFlux-created effects.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: Advanced Effects are separately composed in Chroma Studio.

facts.label: Low-battery notification
notes: The keyboard flashes a red low-battery notification at this threshold.

facts.label: On-the-fly macros
facts.value: Supported with four dedicated macro keys

facts.label: Media controls
facts.value: Multi-function roller with three dedicated media keys

facts.label: Onboard profiles
notes: Razer documents onboard-memory features as unavailable over Bluetooth.

facts.label: Polling rate
notes: 4000 Hz requires the Razer HyperPolling Wireless Dongle.

facts.label: Power Saving Mode
facts.value: Reduces polling, turns keyboard lighting off, and pauses Synapse connectivity
notes: This mode is distinct from ordinary idle sleep.

facts.label: Reported USB product IDs
notes: These are reviewed community captures, not a HyperFlux receiver identity.

gaps.title: No exact pinned Linux code record
gaps.detail: Neither pinned OpenRazer nor pinned OpenRGB contains an exact model record. Product documentation and a reviewed support report exist, but no setting implementation is promoted from that absence.

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: The official candidate name does not provide carrier maps, command semantics, or physical proof for this exact model.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 980 hours in Power Saving Mode
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims and depend on reduced polling, disabled lighting, and paused Synapse connectivity.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: No Chroma Studio, limited Chroma effects, and no onboard-memory support
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: On-device lighting shortcuts
notes: These shortcuts are product behavior, not HyperFlux-created effects.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: Advanced Effects are separately composed in Chroma Studio.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: Razer Chroma RGB
notes: The exact 6 by 18 OpenRGB application matrix is retained separately in the pinned implementation record; product documentation does not establish that geometry.

facts.label: Low-battery notification
notes: The keyboard flashes a red low-battery notification at this threshold.

facts.label: Macro workflow
facts.value: Source conflict: the specification table says No, while the same support page and manual describe macro recording workflows
notes: No Boolean capability is promoted until the manufacturer documentation is reconciled.

facts.label: Media controls
facts.value: Multi-function roller and media button

facts.label: Onboard profiles
notes: Razer documents onboard-memory features as unavailable over Bluetooth.

facts.label: Polling rate
notes: 4000 Hz requires the Razer HyperPolling Wireless Dongle.

facts.label: Power policy
facts.value: Configurable dim and sleep timeouts plus a reduced-power mode
notes: The manual describes reduced-power behavior separately from ordinary sleep.

facts.label: Power Saving Mode
facts.value: Reduces polling, turns keyboard lighting off, and pauses Synapse connectivity
notes: This mode is distinct from ordinary idle sleep.

facts.label: Key assignment
facts.value: Source conflict: the specification table says No, while the same support page and manual describe key remapping
notes: No Boolean capability is promoted until the manufacturer documentation is reconciled.

facts.label: Reported USB product IDs
notes: The pinned OpenRGB records independently use the same pair; this does not create HyperFlux authority.

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: OpenRGB has exact wired and vendor-receiver presentation records, but no HyperFlux child profile authorizes this model.

gaps.title: Macro support wording conflicts
gaps.detail: The official specifications say on-the-fly macro recording is absent, while official procedure text and the manual describe macro controls. The catalog preserves the conflict.

gaps.title: Programmability wording conflicts
gaps.detail: The official specifications say fully programmable keys are absent, while official procedure text and the manual describe remapping. The catalog preserves the conflict.

facts.label: Battery and charging indicator
facts.value: Solid red while charging, solid green when full; unplugged status uses green, yellow, or blinking red
notes: This describes the keyboard's own power indicator, not HyperFlux telemetry.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 200 hours with lighting off; official examples range from about 6 to 25 hours with white or Spectrum Cycling lighting
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims, not live capacity telemetry; effect, color, and brightness materially change runtime.

facts.label: Bluetooth Quick Effects
notes: Studio, Audio Visualizer, and onboard-memory lighting are unavailable over Bluetooth.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: No Chroma Studio, Audio Visualizer, or onboard-memory lighting; only a six-effect Quick Effects subset is available
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: Form factor
facts.value: 65% wireless hot-swappable keyboard with retained arrow keys

facts.label: Official HyperFlux role
facts.value: Compatible HyperSpeed keyboard for multi-device connectivity; the mat does not charge keyboards
notes: Manufacturer compatibility does not provide Linux carrier maps, command semantics, or physical qualification for this model.

facts.label: On-device lighting shortcuts
notes: Static, Breathing, Reactive, and Starlight cycle options when their shortcut is pressed again; Wave changes direction.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Brightness is adjustable; lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: Advanced Effects are separately composed in Chroma Studio; this list is product behavior, not HyperFlux-created effects.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: Customizable per-key Razer Chroma RGB with 16.8 million colors
notes: The pinned OpenRazer record models a 5 by 14 matrix; that implementation geometry remains separate from manufacturer documentation.

facts.label: Critical-battery indication
notes: Below this level, the keyboard repeats a blinking-red sequence until depleted or charging.

facts.label: On-the-fly macro recording
notes: Razer states that Synapse must be installed and running for on-the-fly recording.

facts.label: Media controls
facts.value: Function-layer volume and media keys; no dedicated media controls

facts.label: Onboard profiles
notes: Razer documents onboard-memory features as unavailable over Bluetooth.

facts.label: Polling rate
notes: The support page also describes a 1 ms wireless response rate.

facts.label: Wireless power policy
facts.value: Configurable battery-powered dim and sleep timeouts

facts.label: Key rollover
facts.value: N-key rollover with anti-ghosting

facts.label: Snap Tap
notes: The product page documents configuration through Synapse 4.

facts.label: Hot-swappable switches
facts.value: Standard 3-pin and 5-pin mechanical switches

gaps.title: Charging-duration claims conflict
gaps.detail: The official product page says a full charge takes just under five hours, while the linked official master guide says approximately two hours. No single duration is promoted.

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: Razer lists the keyboard for HyperFlux multi-device connectivity and OpenRazer has exact direct-device records, but no receiver-backed HyperFlux child profile authorizes this model.

gaps.title: The linked guide carries a V3 title
gaps.detail: The V4 Mini support page links a PDF whose URL names V4 but whose embedded title and cover say BlackWidow V3 Mini HyperSpeed. Capability claims are cross-checked against the V4 support and product pages.

gaps.title: Official regional wording differs
gaps.detail: The support article labels the model China-exclusive, while an official Canadian product page exists and the US HyperFlux page lists it as compatible. The catalog records compatibility without inferring sales-region availability.

gaps.detail: Exact OpenRazer and OpenRGB records document direct routes, but no receiver-backed HyperFlux profile authorizes this model.

notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

gaps.title: Control count wording conflicts
gaps.detail: The official support introduction says nine customizable controls, while its technical specification says seven programmable buttons. The catalog preserves both statements and does not normalize them into one count.

gaps.title: No exact pinned Linux code record
gaps.detail: The product is documented by Razer, but neither pinned OpenRazer nor pinned OpenRGB contains an exact record. No settings or packet behavior are inferred from a similar Cobra model.

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: Official compatibility with the mat does not provide a receiver carrier map or authorize commands for this exact model.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 100 hours over 2.4 GHz; up to 170 hours over Bluetooth
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims, not live capacity telemetry.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: Synapse polling is limited to 125 Hz, calibration is disabled, and onboard profiles are stored from the 2.4 GHz route
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: Default DPI stages
notes: Stages are customizable in Razer Synapse.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Display-off and idle switch-off plus wireless Smart Dimming
notes: Smart Dimming lowers wireless lighting while the mouse is moving and returns to normal brightness after three seconds of inactivity.

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: Advanced Effects are separately composed in Chroma Studio.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: 11 zones: scroll wheel, logo, and 9-zone multi-zone underglow
notes: The 9-zone underglow count is independently represented by the pinned OpenRGB topology.

facts.label: Low-battery notification
notes: The scroll wheel repeats a red notification below this threshold.

facts.label: Polling rate
notes: 8000 Hz requires the Mouse Dock Pro and HyperPolling Wireless Dongle.

facts.label: Sensor calibration
facts.value: Surface calibration, Smart Tracking, asymmetric cut-off, and lift-off/landing distances
notes: Razer documents calibration as unavailable through Bluetooth.

facts.label: Sleep timeout
facts.value: Configurable wireless idle timeout

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: Direct-route Linux records are useful for presentation and planning, but no exact receiver-backed HyperFlux child profile authorizes this model.

gaps.title: Keyboard settings are not HyperFlux-mapped
gaps.detail: Per-key lighting and passive battery/identity telemetry are qualified. Polling, game mode, macros, remapping, and Bluetooth configuration remain product documentation rather than receiver controls.

facts.label: Advertised battery life
facts.value: Up to 150 hours over 2.4 GHz; up to 300 hours over Bluetooth
notes: Battery-life figures are manufacturer test claims, not live capacity telemetry.

facts.label: Bluetooth limitations
facts.value: Synapse polling is limited to 125 Hz, calibration is disabled, and onboard profiles are stored from the 2.4 GHz route
notes: Bluetooth bypasses the HyperFlux receiver and mat.

facts.label: Default DPI stages
notes: Stages are customizable in Razer Synapse.

facts.label: Lighting power policy
facts.value: Lighting can switch off with the display or after idle time

facts.label: Synapse Quick Effects
notes: Advanced Effects are separately composed in Chroma Studio.

facts.label: Lighting topology
facts.value: 2 user-facing zones: logo and 12-button side plate
notes: Pinned OpenRGB also presents two slots; pinned OpenRazer declares a three-slot matrix, so the source disagreement remains visible.

facts.label: Low-battery notification
notes: The scroll wheel repeats a red notification below this threshold.

facts.label: Profile storage
facts.value: 4 onboard plus 1 active profile
notes: Razer documents onboard profiles as based on the 2.4 GHz route when Bluetooth is used.

facts.label: Programmable controls
facts.value: 19+1 buttons across three interchangeable side plates

facts.label: Scroll behavior
facts.value: Four-way HyperScroll Pro wheel with six configurable scroll modes

facts.label: Tracking calibration
facts.value: Advanced lift-off and landing-distance customization
notes: Razer documents calibration as unavailable through Bluetooth.

facts.label: Sleep timeout
facts.value: Configurable wireless idle timeout

gaps.title: HyperFlux transport is not qualified
gaps.detail: Extensive direct-route control vocabulary exists, but no receiver-backed HyperFlux child profile authorizes this model.

gaps.title: Linux lighting slot count disagrees
gaps.detail: OpenRGB and Razer product documentation describe two user-facing zones, while OpenRazer declares a three-slot matrix. The third slot is not guessed or promoted.
